description: Pharmacy refill escalation meeting. Should produce stories around medication-refill push notifications and household-account verification. Expect a HIPAA conflict (surfacing medication names in notifications) and a duplicate against NS-176 (Pharmacy refill SMS reminder).

deterministic_metrics.name: acceptance_criteria_well_formed
observations: 4/4 stories have AC count in range

deterministic_metrics.name: expected_duplicates_found
observations: Expected duplicate (['sms', 'refill', 'reminder'] ↔ NS-176) not found at confidence ≥ medium

deterministic_metrics.name: expected_constraint_conflicts_found
observations: Expected conflict for story keywords ['medication', 'name', 'notification', 'push'] not flagged

reasons.ac_quality: Most acceptance criteria are concrete and testable with clear Given/When/Then structure, measurable outcomes (5-minute SLA, 10-minute propagation), and observable states, though a few could be more specific about negative-case verification.
reasons.priority_justification: Two high-priority stories (ST-01, ST-02) have zero-length priority_rationale fields despite guardrail warnings, and the Medium priorities lack explicit justification beyond implicit sequencing or Legal review dependencies.
reasons.story_granularity: Stories are well-sized with single, coherent deliverables—each addresses one notification capability or compliance dimension—though ST-04 is somewhat meta (elevating an existing story) rather than a pure delivery item.
reasons.tag_accuracy: Tags are appropriate and consistently applied across the epic (pharmacy, mobile-app, compliance, notifications, sms), though guardrail findings correctly note 'notifications' and 'sms' are non-canonical and should be added to the vocabulary.
reasons.conflict_reasoning: The single flagged conflict (ST-02 vs C-09) is identified as 'low severity' and labeled as addressing a gap rather than a true conflict, which is reasonable but slightly equivocates—if C-09 mandates verified delivery and ST-02 implements it, there is no conflict at all.

llm_judge.overall_comment: This is a strong backlog synthesis with well-structured, testable stories and thoughtful compliance integration. The primary weakness is missing priority rationale for high-priority items, which undermines sequencing confidence and stakeholder communication; adding explicit business or risk justification would significantly strengthen delivery planning.
